claims.claim: Deepgram: frame sizes in the 20-50 millisecond range strike a balance between reactivity and network efficiency; smaller frames increase responsiveness but raise overhead
claims.found: 'Audio should be streamed in small, consistent frames rather than buffered in large chunks. Frame sizes in the 20-50 millisecond range strike a balance between reactivity and network efficiency. Smaller frames increase responsiveness but raise overhead; larger frames reduce overhead but introduce audible lag.' (lines 509-513)
'Re-architect everything to handle audio in 20ms chunks instead of multi-second files.' and 'Processes audio in 20ms chunks as it arrives' (vapi-pipeline-part-1.md lines 18, 24)

summary: All four code_refs and both source figures verified against ground truth. Pipecat output default 40ms (4x10ms), WebRTC 10ms grain with multiple-of-10ms enforcement, LiveKit 50ms (sample_rate//20), Deepgram 20-50ms quote exact, Vapi 20ms exact. Minor: source file is named vapi-pipeline-part-1.md (note cites shortname vapi-pipeline-1) but content is fully supported.
